devanagari: श्रीकृष्णबाणासुरसंग्रामः तत्र माहेश्वरज्वरेण । माहेश्वरेण च कृता भगवत् स्तुतिः । श्रीशुक उवाच । अपश्यतां चानिरुद्धं तद्बन्धूनां च भारत ...
english_devnagari: śrīkṛṣṇabāṇāsurasaṃgrāmaḥ tatra māheśvarajvareṇamāheśvareṇa ca kṛtā bhagavat stutiḥśrīśuka uvācaapaśyatāṃ cāniruddhaṃ tadbandhūnāṃ ca bhāratacatvāro vārṣikā māsā vyatīyuranuśocatām
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
And thus passed away the four months of the rainy season, while the relatives of Aniruddha, (being unable to trace him) missed him and were grieving for him (for his disappearance), O descendant of Bharata!

devanagari: नारदात् तदुपाकर्ण्य वार्तां बद्धस्य कर्म च । प्रययुः शोणितपुरं वृष्णयः कृष्णदैवताः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: nāradāt tadupākarṇya vārtāṃ baddhasya karma caprayayuḥ śoṇitapuraṃ vṛṣṇayaḥ kṛṣṇadaivatāḥ
translation: Learning from Nārada, the report of Aniruḍdha’s valorous fight and his imprisonment, Vṛṣṇis who looked upon Kṛṣṇa as their presiding deity, speedily marched against Śoṇitapura, the capital of Bāṇa.

devanagari: प्रद्युम्नो युयुधानश्च गदः साम्बोऽथ सारणः । नन्दोपनन्दभद्राद्या रामकृष्णानुवर्तिनः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: pradyumno yuyudhānaśca gadaḥ sāmbo'tha sāraṇaḥnandopanandabhadrādyā rāmakṛṣṇānuvartinaḥ
translation: Pradyumna, Yuyudhāna, Gada, Sāmba and Sāraṇa, Nanda, Upananda, Bhadra and others—all loyal followers of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa—these prominent Sātvata heroes accompanied with twelve akṣauhiṇīs of army, laid close siege to the city of Bāṇa, on all sides.

devanagari: अक्षौहिणीभिर्द्वादशभिः समेताः सर्वतो दिशम् । रुरुधुर्बाणनगरं समन्तात् सात्वतर्षभाः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: akṣauhiṇībhirdvādaśabhiḥ sametāḥ sarvato diśamrurudhurbāṇanagaraṃ samantāt sātvatarṣabhāḥ
translation: Pradyumna, Yuyudhāna, Gada, Sāmba and Sāraṇa, Nanda, Upananda, Bhadra and others—all loyal followers of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa—these prominent Sātvata heroes accompanied with twelve akṣauhiṇīs of army, laid close siege to the city of Bāṇa, on all sides.

devanagari: भज्यमानपुरोद्यान प्राकाराट्टालगोपुरम् । प्रेक्षमाणो रुषाविष्टः तुल्यसैन्योऽभिनिर्ययौ ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhajyamānapurodyāna prākārāṭṭālagopuramprekṣamāṇo ruṣāviṣṭaḥ tulyasainyo'bhiniryayau
translation: Noticing that the parks of the city, fortifications, gates, balconies and towers were shattered down, Bāṇa was fired with rage and taking with him an equally strong army, sallied forth out of the city.

devanagari: बाणार्थे भगवान् रुद्रः ससुतः प्रमथैर्वृतः । आरुह्य नन्दिवृषभं युयुधे रामकृष्णयोः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: bāṇārthe bhagavān rudraḥ sasutaḥ pramathairvṛtaḥāruhya nandivṛṣabhaṃ yuyudhe rāmakṛṣṇayoḥ
translation: The worshipful god Rudra surrounded by the Pramathas and accompanied by his son Skanda, rode his big bull and fought with Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa for the sake of Bāṇa.

devanagari: आसीत् सुतुमुलं युद्धं अद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम् । कृष्णशङ्करयो राजन् प्रद्युम्नगुहयोरपि ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: āsīt sutumulaṃ yuddhaṃ adbhutaṃ romaharṣaṇamkṛṣṇaśaṅkarayo rājan pradyumnaguhayorapi
translation: The combat that raged between Kṛṣṇa and Śaṅkara and the duel between Pradyumna and Guha (Kārttikeya) was tumultuous, astounding and horripilating (making the hair stand on the ends).

devanagari: कुम्भाण्डकूपकर्णाभ्यां बलेन सह संयुगः । साम्बस्य बाणपुत्रेण बाणेन सह सात्यकेः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: kumbhāṇḍakūpakarṇābhyāṃ balena saha saṃyugaḥsāmbasya bāṇaputreṇa bāṇena saha sātyakeḥ
translation: There was an encounter between Balarāma with Kumbhāṇḍa and Kūpakarṇa; and a duel between Sāmba and the son of Bāṇa, and between Bāṇa and Sātyaki.

devanagari: ब्रह्मादयः सुराधीशा मुनयः सिद्धचारणाः । गन्धर्वाप्सरसो यक्षा विमानैर्द्रष्टुमागमन् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmādayaḥ surādhīśā munayaḥ siddhacāraṇāḥgandharvāpsaraso yakṣā vimānairdraṣṭumāgaman
translation: Great gods like Brahmā and others, sages, Siddhas, Cāraṇas, Gandharvas (celestial singers) heavenly damsels, Yakṣas came in their aerial cars to witness the wonderful battle.

devanagari: शङ्करानुचरान् शौरिः भूतप्रमथगुह्यकान् । डाकिनीर्यातुधानांश्च वेतालान् सविनायकान् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: śaṅkarānucarān śauriḥ bhūtapramathaguhyakānḍākinīryātudhānāṃśca vetālān savināyakān
translation: With sharp-pointed shafts discharged from his Śārṅga bow, Kṛṣṇa put to flight the followers of god Śiva viz. goblins, Pramathas, Yakṣas, Dākinīs, Yātudhānas, Vināyakas, Vetālas, and ghosts, Mātṛkas, Piśācas Kūṣmāṇḍas and Brahmarākṣasas.

devanagari: प्रेतमातृपिशाचांश्च कुष्माण्डान् ब्रह्मराक्षसान् । द्रावयामास तीक्ष्णाग्रैः शरैः शार्ङ्गधनुश्च्युतैः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: pretamātṛpiśācāṃśca kuṣmāṇḍān brahmarākṣasāndrāvayāmāsa tīkṣṇāgraiḥ śaraiḥ śārṅgadhanuścyutaiḥ
translation: With sharp-pointed shafts discharged from his Śārṅga bow, Kṛṣṇa put to flight the followers of god Śiva viz. goblins, Pramathas, Yakṣas, Dākinīs, Yātudhānas, Vināyakas, Vetālas, and ghosts, Mātṛkas, Piśācas Kūṣmāṇḍas and Brahmarākṣasas.

devanagari: पृथग्विधानि प्रायुङ्क्त पिणाक्यस्त्राणि शाङ्र्गिणे । प्रत्यस्त्रैः शमयामास शार्ङ्गपाणिरविस्मितः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛthagvidhāni prāyuṅkta piṇākyastrāṇi śāṅrgiṇepratyastraiḥ śamayāmāsa śārṅgapāṇiravismitaḥ
translation: God Śiva, the wielḍer of Pināka bow, discharged various kinds of missiles on Kṛṣṇa, the wielder of Śārṅga bow. Śrī Kṛṣṇa, without expressing any surprise, neutralised each and every missile of Śiva by his counter-missiles.

devanagari: ब्रह्मास्त्रस्य च ब्रह्मास्त्रं वायव्यस्य च पार्वतम् । आग्नेयस्य च पार्जन्यं नैजं पाशुपतस्य च ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmāstrasya ca brahmāstraṃ vāyavyasya ca pārvatamāgneyasya ca pārjanyaṃ naijaṃ pāśupatasya ca
translation: He nullified Brahmāstra with a Brahmāstra, Vāyavyāstra (missile creating stormy winds) with Parvatāstra (a missile surrounding the winds by mountain-like obstacles), the Agnyastra (the fire missile) with the rain missile and Rudra’s special Pāśupata missile with his own Nārāyaṇāstra.

devanagari: मोहयित्वा तु गिरिशं जृम्भणास्त्रेण जृम्भितम् । बाणस्य पृतनां शौरिः जघानासिगदेषुभिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: mohayitvā tu giriśaṃ jṛmbhaṇāstreṇa jṛmbhitambāṇasya pṛtanāṃ śauriḥ jaghānāsigadeṣubhiḥ
translation: Then Śrī Kṛṣṇa stupefied god Śiva by making him yawn and lethargic with Jṛmbhaṇāstra (yawning missile) and in the meanwhile slaughtered Bāṇa’s army with his sword, mace and arrows.

devanagari: स्कन्दः प्रद्युम्नबाणौघैः अर्द्यमानः समन्ततः । असृग् विमुञ्चन् गात्रेभ्यः शिखिनापक्रमद् रणात् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: skandaḥ pradyumnabāṇaughaiḥ ardyamānaḥ samantataḥasṛg vimuñcan gātrebhyaḥ śikhināpakramad raṇāt
translation: Skanda being pierced on all sides by the streams of arrows discharged by Pradyumna and bleeding in every part of the body, retreated from the battle on the back of his peacock.

devanagari: कुम्भाण्डः कूपकर्णश्च पेततुर्मुषलार्दितौ । दुद्रुवुस्तदनीकनि हतनाथानि सर्वतः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kumbhāṇḍaḥ kūpakarṇaśca petaturmuṣalārditaududruvustadanīkani hatanāthāni sarvataḥ
translation: Kumbhāṇḍa and Kūpakarṇa fell down pounded by the pestle of Balarāma and their armies with their leaders killed, raṅ away in all directions.

devanagari: विशीर्यमाणं स्वबलं दृष्ट्वा बाणोऽत्यमर्षणः । कृष्णं अभ्यद्रवत् संख्ये रथी हित्वैव सात्यकिम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: viśīryamāṇaṃ svabalaṃ dṛṣṭvā bāṇo'tyamarṣaṇaḥkṛṣṇaṃ abhyadravat saṃkhye rathī hitvaiva sātyakim
translation: Noticing his army shattered and scattered in this way, Bāṇa became extremely enraged. He left his combatant Sātyaki, and in his chariot he rode to and attacked Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: धनूंष्याकृष्य युगपद् बाणः पञ्चशतानि वै । एकैकस्मिन् शरौ द्वौ द्वौ सन्दधे रणदुर्मदः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: dhanūṃṣyākṛṣya yugapad bāṇaḥ pañcaśatāni vaiekaikasmin śarau dvau dvau sandadhe raṇadurmadaḥ
translation: Simultaneously bending five hundred bows, Bāṇa who was over-proud of his valour, applied a couple of arrows each on each of the bows.

devanagari: तानि चिच्छेद भगवान् धनूंसि युगपद्धरिः । सारथिं रथमश्वांश्च हत्वा शङ्खमपूरयत् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tāni ciccheda bhagavān dhanūṃsi yugapaddhariḥsārathiṃ rathamaśvāṃśca hatvā śaṅkhamapūrayat
translation: The glorious Lord Hari simultaneously snapped all Bāṇa’s bows, killed his charioteer, horses and shattered his chariot and blew his conch.

devanagari: तन्माता कोटरा नाम नग्ना मक्तशिरोरुहा । पुरोऽवतस्थे कृष्णस्य पुत्रप्राणरिरक्षया ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tanmātā koṭarā nāma nagnā maktaśiroruhāpuro'vatasthe kṛṣṇasya putraprāṇarirakṣayā
translation: Bāṇa had a mother[1] (mother-like presiding deity) called Koṭarā. With a desire to protect the life of her son, she appeared before Kṛṣṇa completely nude with locks of her hair dishevelled.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The name of Bāṇa’s real mother was Aśanā. VT clarifies that accord­ing to V.P. Koṭarī was the family deity of Daityas.

devanagari: ततस्तिर्यङ्मुखो नग्नां अनिरीक्षन् गदाग्रजः । बाणश्च तावद् विरथः छिन्नधन्वाविशत् पुरम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tatastiryaṅmukho nagnāṃ anirīkṣan gadāgrajaḥbāṇaśca tāvad virathaḥ chinnadhanvāviśat puram
translation: Then (in that situation), Kṛṣṇa turned his face without casting a look at the naked deity. In the meanwhile, Bāṇa whose chariot was broken and bows cut down, retreated to his capital.

devanagari: विद्राविते भूतगणे ज्वरस्तु त्रीशिरास्त्रिपात् । अभ्यधावत दाशार्हं दहन्निव दिशो दश ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: vidrāvite bhūtagaṇe jvarastu trīśirāstripātabhyadhāvata dāśārhaṃ dahanniva diśo daśa
translation:
When all the army of Bhūtas (goblins etc.) fled away, Jvara (Śiva’s fever) having three heads and three feet, rushed at Kṛṣṇa burning down as it were, all the ten cardinal points.
22(A). And he appeared in the battle in front of Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: अथ नारायणः देवः तं दृष्ट्वा व्यसृजज्ज्वरम् । माहेश्वरो वैष्णवश्च युयुधाते ज्वरावुभौ ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: atha nārāyaṇaḥ devaḥ taṃ dṛṣṭvā vyasṛjajjvarammāheśvaro vaiṣṇavaśca yuyudhāte jvarāvubhau
translation: Thereupon the refulgent god Nārāyaṇa had a look at that Jvara and let loose against him his own (Vaiṣṇava) Fever. In this way the Śaivite and Vaiṣṇavite fevers fought with each other.

devanagari: माहेश्वरः समाक्रन्दन् वैष्णवेन बलार्दितः । अलब्ध्वाभयमन्यत्र भीतो माहेश्वरो ज्वरः । शरणार्थी हृषीकेशं तुष्टाव प्रयताञ्जलिः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: māheśvaraḥ samākrandan vaiṣṇavena balārditaḥalabdhvābhayamanyatra bhīto māheśvaro jvaraḥśaraṇārthī hṛṣīkeśaṃ tuṣṭāva prayatāñjaliḥ
translation: Severely beaten by the fever commanded by Viṣṇu, the Śaivite fever screamed loudly. He could not find any shelter from fear. Being terribly afraid, the fever commanded by Śiva sought refuge with Kṛṣṇa and folding his palm praised him.

devanagari: ज्वर उवाच । नमामि त्वानन्तशक्तिं परेशं । सर्वात्मानं केवलं ज्ञप्तिमात्रम् । विश्वोत्पत्तिस्थानसंरोधहेतुं । यत्तद् ब्रह्म ब्रह्मलिङ्गं ...
english_devnagari: jvara uvācanamāmi tvānantaśaktiṃ pareśaṃsarvātmānaṃ kevalaṃ jñaptimātramviśvotpattisthānasaṃrodhahetuṃyattad brahma brahmaliṅgaṃ praśāntam
translation:
Jvara prayed:
I bow to you, Lord of infinite potency, the controller of great gods (like Brahmā and others), the Soul of all (the universe). One without a second, (of the nature of) Absolute Consciousness, the Cause of the creation, subsistence and dissolution of the universe, you are what is known as the Brahman which is indicated by the Vedas (but not cognised directly), absolutely serene, subject to no modification.

devanagari: कालो दैवं कर्म जीवः स्वभावो । द्रव्यं क्षेत्रं प्राण आत्मा विकारः । तत्सङ्घातो बीजरोहप्रवाहः । त्वन्मायैषा तन्निषेधं प्रपद्ये ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: kālo daivaṃ karma jīvaḥ svabhāvodravyaṃ kṣetraṃ prāṇa ātmā vikāraḥtatsaṅghāto bījarohapravāhaḥtvanmāyaiṣā tanniṣedhaṃ prapadye
translation: (You, being beyond all attributes, are the Lord of all). The Time-spirit (that impels action), Providence (resultant from the karma or action), Karma, Svabhāva (the natural propensities as a result of Karma), substance (subtle elements), the body, Prāṇa (vital breath called Sūtra), the Ego, modifications (viz. eleven senses—10 cognative and cognitive senses + 1 mind, the internal organ) and the combination of these evolutes called the Liṅga or subtle body which sets in motion the stream of cause-effect relation like that of the seed and the tree—all is your Māyā. I seek shelter in you in whom there is negation of Māyā.

devanagari: नानाभावैर्लीलयैवोपपन्नैः । देवान् साधून् लोकसेतून्बिभर्षि । हंस्युन्मार्गान् हिंसया वर्तमानान् । जन्मैतत्ते भारहाराय भूमेः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: nānābhāvairlīlayaivopapannaiḥdevān sādhūn lokasetūnbibharṣihaṃsyunmārgān hiṃsayā vartamānānjanmaitatte bhārahārāya bhūmeḥ
translation: Sportively assuming various incarnations (as Fish, Tortoise, Boar etc.) you protect the gods, saintly persons who abide by the dharma (duties prescribed for their respective class and stage in life) and for their protection you exterminate those who deviate from the righteous path and live by committing violence. This incarnation of yours is intended for lessening the burden of the earth.

devanagari: तप्तोऽहं ते तेजसा दुःसहेन । शान्तोग्रेणात्युल्बणेन ज्वरेण । तावत्तापो देहिनां तेऽङ्घ्रिमूलं । नो सेवेरन् यावदाशानुबद्धाः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tapto'haṃ te tejasā duḥsahenaśāntogreṇātyulbaṇena jvareṇatāvattāpo dehināṃ te'ṅghrimūlaṃno severan yāvadāśānubaddhāḥ
translation: I am being consumed by the extremely unbearable, terrible, deadly cold fever created by your irresistible energy. Corporeal beings have to suffer, so long as they, remaining enmeshed in desires, do not take shelter at the soles of your feet.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । त्रिशिरस्ते प्रसन्नोऽस्मि व्येतु ते मज्ज्वराद्भयम् । यो नौ स्मरति संवादं तस्य त्वन्न भवेद्भयम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācatriśiraste prasanno'smi vyetu te majjvarādbhayamyo nau smarati saṃvādaṃ tasya tvanna bhavedbhayam
translation:
The Lord said:
“Oh Triśira! (three-headed fever) I am pleased with you. May your fear from my fear be set at rest. May he who remembers our conversation, be free from fear from you.”

devanagari: इत्युक्तोऽच्युतमानम्य गतो माहेश्वरो ज्वरः । बाणस्तु रथमारूढः प्रागाद्योत्स्यञ्जनार्दनम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: ityukto'cyutamānamya gato māheśvaro jvaraḥbāṇastu rathamārūḍhaḥ prāgādyotsyañjanārdanam
translation: Thus ordered the Śaivite Fever bowed down to Lord Kṛṣṇa and departed. But Bāṇa mounting his chariot advanced and fought with Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: ततो बाहुसहस्रेण नानायुधधरोऽसुरः । मुमोच परमक्रुद्धो बाणांश्चक्रायुधे नृप ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tato bāhusahasreṇa nānāyudhadharo'suraḥmumoca paramakruddho bāṇāṃścakrāyudhe nṛpa
translation: Then equipped with various weapons in each of his arm, Bāṇa who was extremely enraged, discharged a volley of arrows on the Lord with the discus as his weapon, O king!

devanagari: तस्यास्यतोऽस्त्राण्यसकृत् चक्रेण क्षुरनेमिना । चिच्छेद भगवान्बाहून् शाखा इव वनस्पतेः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāsyato'strāṇyasakṛt cakreṇa kṣuranemināciccheda bhagavānbāhūn śākhā iva vanaspateḥ
translation: White Bāṇa was discharging missiles, the Lord amputated with his discus, the sharp razor-like felly, the arms of Bāṇa, like branches of a tree.

devanagari: बाहुषु छिद्यमानेषु बाणस्य भगवान् भवः । भक्तानकम्प्युपव्रज्य चक्रायुधमभाषत ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: bāhuṣu chidyamāneṣu bāṇasya bhagavān bhavaḥbhaktānakampyupavrajya cakrāyudhamabhāṣata
translation: While Bāṇa’s arms were thus being lopped off, the worshipful god Śiva who was compassionate to his devotee approached Kṛṣṇa and requested him as follows:

devanagari: श्रीरुद्र उवाच । त्वं हि ब्रह्म परं ज्योतिः गूढं ब्रह्मणि वाङ्मये । यं पश्यन्त्यमलात्मान आकाशमिव केवलम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrudra uvācatvaṃ hi brahma paraṃ jyotiḥ gūḍhaṃ brahmaṇi vāṅmayeyaṃ paśyantyamalātmāna ākāśamiva kevalam
translation:
Rudra submitted:
(It is not surprising that Bāṇa fights with you as he is unaware of your real nature, for you are the veritable Brahman, the Supreme Light lying concealed in the Vedas (which are the Brahman revealed in verbal form). You reveal yourself to sages and hence those sages of pure heart realize you to be all-pervading and pure as the sky and (eternally) absolute.

devanagari: नाभिर्नभोऽग्निर्मुखमम्बु रेतो । द्यौः शीर्षमाशाः श्रुतिरङ्घ्रिरुर्वी । चन्द्रो मनो यस्य दृगर्क आत्मा । अहं समुद्रो जठरं भुजेन्द्रः ॥ ३...
english_devnagari: nābhirnabho'gnirmukhamambu retodyauḥ śīrṣamāśāḥ śrutiraṅghrirurvīcandro mano yasya dṛgarka ātmāahaṃ samudro jaṭharaṃ bhujendraḥ
translation: You are indeed the Cosmic Man (Virāṭ puruṣa) that comprise within you (as parts of your body) all the universe. Thus the sky is your navel; the fire, your mouth; water, your seminal fluid; the heavenly region, your head; the cardinal points, your ears; the earth, your feet; the moon your mind; the Sun, your eye; myself (viz. Rudra), your Ego (ahaṃkāra); the seas, your belly; Indra (and other protectors of the world), your arms;

devanagari: रोमाणि यस्यौषधयोऽम्बुवाहाः । केशा विरिञ्चो धिषणा विसर्गः । प्रजापतिर्हृदयं यस्य धर्मः । स वै भवान् पुरुषो लोककल्पः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: romāṇi yasyauṣadhayo'mbuvāhāḥkeśā viriñco dhiṣaṇā visargaḥprajāpatirhṛdayaṃ yasya dharmaḥsa vai bhavān puruṣo lokakalpaḥ
translation: Herbs and plants are your hair on the body; clouds, the locks of your hair; god Brahmā, your intellect; the pro- creator of the worlds, your generative organ; and Dharma (the god upholding righteousness), your heart. (But this Bāṇa does not know this Cosmic Personality of yours).

devanagari: तवावतारोऽयमकुण्ठधामन् । धर्मस्य गुप्त्यै जगतो हिताय । वयं च सर्वे भवतानुभाविता । विभावयामो भुवनानि सप्त ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tavāvatāro'yamakuṇṭhadhāmandharmasya guptyai jagato hitāyavayaṃ ca sarve bhavatānubhāvitāvibhāvayāmo bhuvanāni sapta
translation: This incarnation of yours, O Lord of undiminished glory and power (or who stay eternally in Go-loka), is for the protection of righteousness and for the prosperity of the world. All of us (protectors of various spheres) are delegated powers by you and hence we (can) protect the seven spheres of the universe.

devanagari: त्वमेक आद्यः पुरुषोऽद्वितीयः । तुर्यः स्वदृग् हेतुरहेतुरीशः । प्रतीयसेऽथापि यथाविकारं । स्वमायया सर्वगुणप्रसिद्ध्यै ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: tvameka ādyaḥ puruṣo'dvitīyaḥturyaḥ svadṛg heturaheturīśaḥpratīyase'thāpi yathāvikāraṃsvamāyayā sarvaguṇaprasiddhyai
translation: You are the one without a second, the First Person (beyond all persons belonging to the three states), pure, self-lumiṇous through knowledge, the cause of all but yourself without any cause, the controller (of the universe). Yet you manifest your self through your Māyā as an individual Soul (in every being be it gods, men or subhuman beings) and throw light on everything.

devanagari: यथैव सूर्यः पिहितश्छायया स्वया । छायां च रूपाणि च सञ्चकास्ति । एवं गुणेनापिहितो गुणांस्त्वम् । आत्मप्रदीपो गुणिनश्च भूमन् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: yathaiva sūryaḥ pihitaśchāyayā svayāchāyāṃ ca rūpāṇi ca sañcakāstievaṃ guṇenāpihito guṇāṃstvamātmapradīpo guṇinaśca bhūman
translation: O Perfect Lord! Just as the Sun, covered under his own shadow (viz. clouds, his creation) illuminates both the clouds as well as other forms, you, though the Self-luminous, get concealed under the guṇas (viz. Ego) and yet make all the guṇas and their products shine (with your light).

devanagari: यन्मायामोहितधियः पुत्रदारगृहादिषु । उन्मज्जन्ति निमज्जन्ति प्रसक्ता वृजिनार्णवे ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: yanmāyāmohitadhiyaḥ putradāragṛhādiṣuunmajjanti nimajjanti prasaktā vṛjinārṇave
translation: With their intellect deluded by your Māyā, men are attached to their sons, wives, houses and other belongings and they sink and come up again in the sea of misery (saṃsāra)

devanagari: देवदत्तमिमं लब्ध्वा नृलोकमजितेन्द्रियः । यो नाद्रियेत त्वत्पादौ स शोच्यो ह्यात्मवञ्चकः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: devadattamimaṃ labdhvā nṛlokamajitendriyaḥyo nādriyeta tvatpādau sa śocyo hyātmavañcakaḥ
translation: He who, after obtaining this human body (birth) through the Grace of the God, does not worship respectfully your feet, is certainly a self-deceiver fit to be pitied.

devanagari: यस्त्वां विसृजते मर्त्य आत्मानं प्रियमीश्वरम् । विपर्ययेन्द्रियार्थार्थं विषमत्त्यमृतं त्यजन् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: yastvāṃ visṛjate martya ātmānaṃ priyamīśvaramviparyayendriyārthārthaṃ viṣamattyamṛtaṃ tyajan
translation: The mortal man who, for the sake of sense objects which are worthless and hollow, neglects you who are the beloved Soul and the Ruler of the world, consumes poison throwing aside the nectar.

devanagari: अहं ब्रह्माथ विबुधा मुनयश्चामलाशयाः । सर्वात्मना प्रपन्नास्त्वां आत्मानं प्रेष्ठमीश्वरम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ brahmātha vibudhā munayaścāmalāśayāḥsarvātmanā prapannāstvāṃ ātmānaṃ preṣṭhamīśvaram
translation: I myself, god Brahmā, celestials and sages of pure heart have with all our heart and soul resorted to you who are the most beloved Soul and the controller of the world.

devanagari: तं त्वा जगत्स्थित्युदयान्तहेतुं । समं प्रशान्तं सुहृदात्मदैवम् । अनन्यमेकं जगदात्मकेतं । भवापवर्गाय भजाम देवम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tvā jagatsthityudayāntahetuṃsamaṃ praśāntaṃ suhṛdātmadaivamananyamekaṃ jagadātmaketaṃbhavāpavargāya bhajāma devam
translation: In order to get Liberation from saṃsāra, we seek asylum in you and worship you who are the cause of creation, subsistence and dissolution of the world, equal to all, serene, a beloved friend and deity. You are One without a second, the only goal, the one abode of all the world.

devanagari: अयं ममेष्टो दयितोऽनुवर्ती । मयाभयं दत्तममुष्य देव । संपाद्यतां तद्भवतः प्रसादो । यथा हि ते दैत्यपतौ प्रसादः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ mameṣṭo dayito'nuvartīmayābhayaṃ dattamamuṣya devasaṃpādyatāṃ tadbhavataḥ prasādoyathā hi te daityapatau prasādaḥ
translation: O god, this Bāṇāsura is my dear devotee deeply attached to me and much liked by me. Full protection has been promised by me to him. Therefore, may Your Grace be extended to him as you did in the case of Prahlāḍa, the king of Daityas.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । यदात्थ भगवन् त्वं नः करवाम प्रियं तव । भवतो यद्व्यवसितं तन्मे साध्वनुमोदितम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācayadāttha bhagavan tvaṃ naḥ karavāma priyaṃ tavabhavato yadvyavasitaṃ tanme sādhvanumoditam
translation:
The Lord said:
O worshipful Śiva! We shall comply with whatever you have requested us and what is pleasing to you. Whatever you have decided (about Bāṇa) has my full approval.

devanagari: अवध्योऽयं ममाप्येष वैरोचनिसुतोऽसुरः । प्रह्रादाय वरो दत्तो न वध्यो मे तवान्वयः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: avadhyo'yaṃ mamāpyeṣa vairocanisuto'suraḥprahrādāya varo datto na vadhyo me tavānvayaḥ
translation: This Asura is the son of Bali and as such I should not kill him. I have conferred a boon on Prahlāḍa that none ofhis descendants would be killed by me.

devanagari: दर्पोपशमनायास्य प्रवृक्णा बाहवो मया । सूदितं च बलं भूरि यच्च भारायितं भुवः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: darpopaśamanāyāsya pravṛkṇā bāhavo mayāsūditaṃ ca balaṃ bhūri yacca bhārāyitaṃ bhuvaḥ
translation: It was just to curb his arrogance that his arms have been lopped off by me. And his big army is annihilated because it was a burden to the earth.

devanagari: चत्वारोऽस्य भुजाः शिष्टा भविष्यत्यजरामरः । पार्षदमुख्यो भवतो न कुतश्चिद्भयोऽसुरः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: catvāro'sya bhujāḥ śiṣṭā bhaviṣyatyajarāmaraḥpārṣadamukhyo bhavato na kutaścidbhayo'suraḥ
translation: The four arms that are left to him (Bāṇa) shall not suffer from age or destruction. From no quarters this Asura need entertain any fear and he shall be your attendant.

devanagari: इति लब्ध्वाभयं कृष्णं प्रणम्य शिरसासुरः । प्राद्युम्निं रथमारोप्य सवध्वा समुपानयत् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: iti labdhvābhayaṃ kṛṣṇaṃ praṇamya śirasāsuraḥprādyumniṃ rathamāropya savadhvā samupānayat
translation: Having thus secured protection, the Asura bowed to Kṛṣṇa with his head and brought Aniruddha, the son of Pradyumna along with his bride, placing them in a chariot.

devanagari: अक्षौहिण्या परिवृतं सुवासःसमलङ्कृतम् । सपत्‍नीकं पुरस्कृत्य ययौ रुद्रानुमोदितः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: akṣauhiṇyā parivṛtaṃ suvāsaḥsamalaṅkṛtamsapat‍nīkaṃ puraskṛtya yayau rudrānumoditaḥ
translation: Placing in the van, Aniruddha and his bride who were clad in excellent dress, decked with ornaments and guarded by an akṣauhiṇī of soldiers, Kṛṣṇa set out with compliments from Rudra.

devanagari: स्वराजधानीं समलङ्कृतां ध्वजैः । सतोरणैरुक्षितमार्गचत्वराम् । विवेश शङ्खानकदुन्दुभिस्वनैः । अभ्युद्यतः पौरसुहृद्द्विजातिभिः ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: svarājadhānīṃ samalaṅkṛtāṃ dhvajaiḥsatoraṇairukṣitamārgacatvarāmviveśa śaṅkhānakadundubhisvanaiḥabhyudyataḥ paurasuhṛddvijātibhiḥ
translation: He entered His capital (Dvārakā) which was tastefully decorated with flags and ornamental arches, and had its streets and quadrangles thoroughly besprinkled with water. He was warmly received by the citizens, friends, relatives and Brāhmaṇas, with the blowing of conchs and sounding of drums and kettle-drums.

devanagari: य एवं कृष्णविजयं शङ्करेण च संयुगम् । संस्मरेत् प्रातरुत्थाय न तस्य स्यात् पराजयः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: ya evaṃ kṛṣṇavijayaṃ śaṅkareṇa ca saṃyugamsaṃsmaret prātarutthāya na tasya syāt parājayaḥ
translation:
He who rising early at dawn, recalls and contemplates on the story of combat between Śrī Kṛṣṇa and god Śaṅkara and Kṛṣṇa’s final triumph, will never meet any defeat in life.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । अनिरुद्धानयनं नाम त्रिषष्टितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdheaniruddhānayanaṃ nāma triṣaṣṭitamo'dhyāyaḥ
